mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity valuations and tightens financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack increases, signaling weakening economic momentum and higher recession probability, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer spending and construction activity, generating positive corporate earnings revisions and lifting S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and output growth, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 during recovery phases, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero following industrial production spikes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.0% year-over-year growth threshold, inflation expectations anchor higher, forcing markets to price in prolonged restrictive policy and generating negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation is driven by transitory supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction effect reverses sign during labor market normalization phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding trend levels signals strong aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which translates into sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals yield negative forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the predictive correlation decays to zero when controlling for monetary policy stance.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, simultaneous declines in industrial production and rising inflation indicate stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The stagflation signal produces positive returns in > 60% of late-cycle observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance outside of peak cycle phases.
